narration_text:
Now that we've covered the fundamentals of computer networking, I want you to focus on the importance of acknowledging the work of others in our field. Notice that these slides have been made available to us by professors Kurose and Ross, who are the copyright holders of the material within. They've generously allowed us to use their work in this course, and we're grateful for their contribution.

Let's take a moment to appreciate the effort that goes into creating educational resources like these slides. Edits and additions have been made by my colleague, Mario Reja, PhD, to tailor the material to our Computer Networking course at the Faculty of Informatics, UVT.

Look at the image on your slide – it's the cover of the 9th edition of "Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach" by Jim Kurose and Keith Ross, published by Pearson in 2025. Take a glance at the title and the authors' names – this is the book that these slides are based on. As we continue through the course, remember that the concepts we're learning are built on the foundation laid by pioneers in our field.
